title: श्री मत्स्य स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मत्स्य स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमो भगवते मत्स्यरूपाय।
वेदरक्षकाय जलचराय विष्णवे नमः॥

मत्स्यरूपं हरिं वन्दे वेदरक्षणतत्परम्।
प्रलयार्णवमग्नानां वेदानामुद्धरं प्रभुम्॥ १॥

सत्यव्रतमहाराजरक्षणार्थं जगत्पतिम्।
महामीनस्वरूपेण प्रकटं परमेश्वरम्॥ २॥

हयग्रीवासुरं हत्वा वेदान् प्रत्यानयत् प्रभुः।
ब्रह्मणे प्रददौ वेदान् सृष्ट्यर्थमविकम्पितान्॥ ३॥

शृङ्गवानतिदीप्तश्च स्वर्णवर्णो महाप्रभुः।
मत्स्यरूपी जगन्नाथः सप्तऋषीन् परिरक्षति॥ ४॥

प्रलयोदधिमध्ये तु नौकां बध्वा तु शृङ्गके।
वासुकिं रज्जुमादाय सत्यव्रतमरक्षयत्॥ ५॥

मत्स्यपुराणप्रवक्तारं मनवे ज्ञानदायकम्।
धर्मसंस्थापनं कर्तुमवतीर्णं जनार्दनम्॥ ६॥

प्रथमं दशावताराणां मत्स्यरूपं सनातनम्।
अनन्तकोटिब्रह्माण्डनायकं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

जलचराधिपतिं विष्णुं क्षीरसागरशायिनम्।
मत्स्यरूपधरं देवं शरणं प्रपदे सदा॥ ८॥

चतुर्भुजं शङ्खचक्रगदापद्मधरं हरिम्।
मत्स्यपुच्छसमायुक्तं नारायणमहं भजे॥ ९॥

ओषधीः सर्वबीजानि सप्तऋषीन् मनुमेव च।
रक्षयित्वा प्रलयान्ते नवसृष्टिमकारयत्॥ १०॥

मत्स्यस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेद् भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री मत्स्य स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Matsya Stotram ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Matsya-rupaya |
Veda-rakshakaya Jalacharaya Vishnave Namah ||

Matsya-rupam Harim vande Veda-rakshana-tatparam |
Pralaya-arnava-magnanam Vedanam-uddharam Prabhum || 1 ||

Satyavrata-Maharaja-rakshanartham Jagatpatim |
Maha-mina-svarupena prakatam Parameshwaram || 2 ||

Hayagriva-asuram hatva Vedan pratyaanayat Prabhuh |
Brahmane pradadau Vedan srishtyartham-avikampitan || 3 ||

Shringavan-ati-diptash-cha svarna-varno Maha-Prabhuh |
Matsya-rupi Jagannatah Sapta-rishin parirakshati || 4 ||

Pralaya-udadhi-madhye tu naukam badhva tu shringake |
Vasukim rajjum-adaya Satyavratam-arakshayat || 5 ||

Matsya-Purana-pravaktaram Manave jnana-dayakam |
Dharma-samsthapanam kartum-avatirnam Janardanam || 6 ||

Prathamam Dasha-avataranam Matsya-rupam sanatanam |
Ananta-koti-Brahmanda-nayakam pranamami-aham || 7 ||

Jalachara-adhipatim Vishnum Kshira-sagara-shayinam |
Matsya-rupa-dharam Devam sharanam prapade sada || 8 ||

Chatur-bhujam Shankha-chakra-gada-padma-dharam Harim |
Matsya-puchchha-samayuktam Narayanam-aham bhaje || 9 ||

Oshadhih sarva-bijani Sapta-rishin Manum-eva cha |
Rakshayitva pralaya-ante nava-srishtim-akarayat || 10 ||

Matsya-stotram-idam punyam yah pathed bhakti-samyutah |
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnulokam sa gachchhati || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Matsya Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: वेदरक्षण में तत्पर, प्रलयसागर में डूबे वेदों का उद्धार करने वाले मत्स्यरूपी हरि को वन्दन। सत्यव्रत महाराज की रक्षा हेतु महामीन स्वरूप से प्रकट हुए परमेश्वर को प्रणाम। हयग्रीव असुर को मारकर वेद वापस लाए और ब्रह्मा को सृष्टि हेतु प्रदान किए। स्वर्णवर्ण, शृंगवान, दीप्तिमान मत्स्यरूपी जगन्नाथ ने सप्तर्षियों की रक्षा की। प्रलय सागर में नौका को शृंग से बांधकर, वासुकि को रज्जु बनाकर सत्यव्रत की रक्षा की। यह दशावतार का प्रथम अवतार है। इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से सर्वपाप मुक्ति और विष्णुलोक प्राप्ति होती है।
benefits: Invokes the protective power of Vishnu's first avatar
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants divine protection
Traditionally invoked for protection from floods, water-related dangers, and natural calamities
Invokes knowledge and wisdom as matsya taught the puranas
Invokes the blessing of vishnuloka after death
Traditionally recited to invoke preservation of dharma in one's life
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Matsya Stotram (श्री मत्स्य स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Matsya Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke the protective power of Vishnu's first avatar and protect from floods, water-related dangers, and natural calamities. It is also said to destroy all sins and grant divine protection. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Matsya Jayanti. With 11 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Matsya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Matsya Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Lord Vishnu's Matsya (fish) avatar who saved the Vedas.
relatedSearches: Vedas restored to Brahma Matsya story
First avatar of Vishnu fish meaning
Stotras to recite on Saptami tithi

seoFaqs.question: What episode does Matsya Stotram commemorate?
seoFaqs.answer: Matsya Stotram celebrates Vishnu's first avatar, the great fish (Matsya), who rescued Manu and the seeds of life from the cosmic deluge that ended the previous world cycle. The episode is told in detail in the Bhagavata Purana canto 8, chapter 24 (especially verses 7-58), and also in the Matsya Purana, the Mahabharata's Vana Parva, and the Shatapatha Brahmana. According to the Bhagavata, Vishnu appeared as a small fish in King Satyavrata's hands, grew miraculously, and instructed him to gather the seven sages, the seeds of all plants and the saptarshi-sapta-loka inhabitants on a great boat. He then towed the boat through the deluge using the serpent Vasuki as a rope tied to his horn, and during the journey expounded the Matsya Purana and the essence of the Vedas to the king.

seoFaqs.question: Why did Vishnu choose the form of a fish?
seoFaqs.answer: The fish is the natural form for an avatar whose first task is to navigate a flooded world. Beyond practicality, commentators note that Matsya represents the stage of life that begins entirely in water, the most ancient form of biological existence in the evolutionary sense, and the foundation on which all later land-based avatars rest. The avatar sequence Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Krishna, Buddha and Kalki is therefore sometimes read as a Hindu intuition of evolution from aquatic to amphibian to mammal to human. Matsya, as the first member of the sequence, signals Vishnu's care for life from its very earliest cosmological moment.

seoFaqs.question: What does the stotram say Vishnu taught King Satyavrata during the deluge?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhagavata records that during the long voyage on the deluge waters, Matsya expounded to Satyavrata the essence of the Vedas, the science of self-knowledge, and the teachings later collected as the Matsya Purana. He also restored the Vedas themselves, which had been stolen by the demon Hayagriva and lost during the cosmic dissolution; verse 8.24.61 of the Bhagavata explicitly praises Vishnu as the one who returned the Vedic literature to Brahma when Brahma awakened from his sleep. Matsya Stotram condenses this episode by praising Vishnu as the primordial protector of both biological life (through the boat) and revealed knowledge (through the recovered Vedas).

seoFaqs.question: When is Matsya Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most important occasion is Matsya Jayanti, which falls on the third tithi (tritiya) of the bright half of Chaitra, marking the avatar's appearance. The stotram is also chanted on the saptami tithi of each fortnight, which is associated with Matsya in some traditions, on Vaikuntha Ekadashi as part of the dashavatara sequence, during the Kumbha Mela bathing days when devotees enter the sacred rivers and recall the deluge mythology, and during Pitru Paksha as a prayer for the safe passage of departed ancestors across the waters of samsara. Householders sometimes chant it before crossing seas or undertaking long water journeys.

seoFaqs.question: What contemporary lessons do teachers draw from the Matsya story?
seoFaqs.answer: Three readings are common in contemporary teaching. First, ecological: Matsya gathers seeds and species onto a boat before the flood, which is read as a divine endorsement of conservation and biodiversity. Second, civilisational: the avatar's first work after rescue is to restore knowledge to Brahma, suggesting that culture and wisdom must be deliberately preserved across crises rather than assumed safe. Third, personal: each individual passes through periods of pralaya, that is overwhelming change, in which spiritual practice and right relationships function as the boat that carries one through. The stotram is often recited at moments of personal upheaval as a remembrance that the same Vishnu who guided Manu can guide the devotee.

seoFaqs.question: Where are the principal temples connected to Matsya?
seoFaqs.answer: The most famous Matsya temple is the Matsya Narayana temple at Nagalapuram in Andhra Pradesh, established with the deity in fish form. Veda Narayana temple at Bhadradri and several Vaishnava centres maintain Matsya as part of their dashavatara iconography. Vedanarayana temple at Nagalapuram, attributed to Sri Krishnadevaraya, is particularly noted for its Matsya Jayanti celebrations. Within most major Vishnu temples, including Tirupati, Srirangam and Guruvayur, Matsya is honoured as the first member of the dashavatara in iconographic panels and in the daily recitation of avatar names. Pilgrims to the Kumbha Mela bathing sites also commonly chant Matsya Stotram as part of their snana sankalpa.
